title: Execution Evidence
description: Per-command execution evidence for Goal Runtime tasks. Captures what command ran, where, on which commit, and the SHA-256 of its stdout, enabling tamper-evident reconstruction. Distinct from release-level evidence (release/manifest/latest.json), which aggregates multiple execution evidences.

command.description: Verbatim shell command that was executed, including flags.

cwd.description: Absolute working directory at execution time. May be redacted to a relative repo-root path for portability.

branch.description: Git branch name (or detached HEAD marker) at execution time.

commit.description: Full 40-character lowercase git commit SHA at execution time.

exit_code.description: Process exit code. 0 indicates success; non-zero indicates failure. Failure evidences are still valid and feed Retrospective.

timestamp.description: RFC 3339 timestamp at command completion, with timezone offset.

stdout_sha256.description: Lowercase hex SHA-256 of the captured stdout. The raw stdout is stored under artifact_path.

stderr_sha256.description: Lowercase hex SHA-256 of the captured stderr, when stderr is preserved.

artifact_path.description: Repository-relative path to the persisted artifact (typically under reports/ or release/evidence/).

reconstructed.description: True when the evidence was rebuilt after loss (RULE-EVID-LOSS-002). Release-blocking evidence must not be reconstructed at low confidence.

confidence.description: Confidence level when reconstructed=true. Release evidence requires high.

tags.description: Optional labels such as gate name, task ID, or release ID.
